description: Prompt templates used in the Financial News Source Bias experiment
system_prompt: You are an expert financial news analyst evaluating the credibility of financial news headlines.

blind_prompt.description: Condition A — no source information provided
blind_prompt.template:
Please evaluate the credibility of the following financial news headline.

Headline: "{headline}"

Rate the credibility on a scale from 1 to 5, where:
1 = Very misleading or false
2 = Likely misleading
3 = Uncertain / neutral
4 = Likely credible
5 = Highly credible

Respond in this exact format:
RATING: [number 1-5]
JUSTIFICATION: [one sentence explanation]

source_revealed_prompt.description: Condition B — source name appended to headline
source_revealed_prompt.template:
Please evaluate the credibility of the following financial news headline.

Headline: "{headline}"
Source: This headline was published by {source}.

Rate the credibility on a scale from 1 to 5, where:
1 = Very misleading or false
2 = Likely misleading
3 = Uncertain / neutral
4 = Likely credible
5 = Highly credible

Respond in this exact format:
RATING: [number 1-5]
JUSTIFICATION: [one sentence explanation]

design_notes.why_temperature_0: Temperature set to 0 for reproducibility — identical prompts produce identical outputs
design_notes.why_structured_output: RATING/JUSTIFICATION format enables reliable regex parsing across 500 responses
design_notes.key_design_principle: Only one variable changes between conditions — the source line. Headline, instructions, and rating scale are identical.
